content:
{
  "title": "Cross-lingual Contextual Prompt Injection for Low-Resource Language Tasks",
  "problem": "Current prompting methods for large language models (LLMs) struggle to elicit strong performance on NLP tasks in low-resource languages due to limited data and cross-lingual transfer inefficiencies. There is a need for prompting strategies that can more effectively leverage existing high-resource language capabilities to benefit underrepresented languages.",
  "motivation": "Low-resource languages remain severely underserved by LLMs, despite advances in multilingual transfer. Structural differences and translation artifacts limit standard prompting’s ability to bridge gaps. New methods that make LLMs more aware of and sensitive to multilingual context are essential for equitable NLP progress.",
  "method": "We propose a novel prompting approach that injects dynamic cross-lingual context into LLM prompts by explicitly encoding short, aligned semantic exemplars across multiple languages. For each low-resource task prompt, we retrieve a set of parallel or comparable sentences in high-resource languages with rich annotated data, encode their semantic content using a lightweight alignment module, and integrate these into the prompt as multi-language contextual anchors. The LLM is thus prompted not only with the target low-resource input, but also with concise contextual scaffolding from related high-resource language data—without relying on translation or code-switching.",
  "experiment_plan": "We will evaluate on tasks like named entity recognition and sentiment analysis across several low-resource languages from the XTREME and FLEURS benchmarks. Baselines include vanilla prompts, machine-translated prompts, and retrieval-augmented prompting. Metrics are F1 for classification tasks and BLEU for generation. We will analyze performance and ablation on context selection and alignment strategies."
}
